title: stylidium applanatum
intro: Stylidium applanatum, also known as the flat-stemmed triggerplant, is a rare and unique species of flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used by the Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating wounds, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and antioxidant effects. In herbal preparations, Stylidium applanatum is often used as a decoction or infusion, and it can also be made into a tincture or salve for topical application.
